rows.BaselineTrend: Open smaller from EP, wider/larger from late position; no limping except SB mix.
rows.FrequencyTrend: UTG/LJ ~16-18%, HJ ~21%, CO ~25-28%, BTN ~40-44%, SB ~38-62% (raise-or-fold vs mix).
rows.SizingTrend: ~2bb EP, ~2.3bb CO, ~2.5bb BTN, 3bb SB online; 3bb+ live.
rows.MixedStrategyRegions: Bottom of each range mixes raise/fold (A3s, 55, suited gappers, KTo/T8s thresholds).
rows.RakeSensitivity: Higher rake (NL50) → tighter, more blocker-heavy; lower rake (NL500) → wider.
rows.PopulationDeviation: Micro pools call too wide preflop and 3-bet too tight → open a touch wider for value, c-bet more.
rows.NotesForTPoker: Mark core positions Production-Ready; treat SB as its own profile (CAL-PRE-RFI-SB).

rows.BaselineTrend: Raise-or-fold default OOP; limp/raise mix is a valid alternative vs weak opponents.
rows.FrequencyTrend: SB ~38-62% depending on raise-or-fold vs mix modeling.
rows.MixedStrategyRegions: Whole bottom of range mixes; limp range (if used) is itself a mixed strategy.
rows.RakeSensitivity: Higher rake tightens SB sharply (worst seat for rake).
rows.PopulationDeviation: Vs limpy/passive BBs, a limp-behind/iso mix can beat pure raise-or-fold.
rows.NotesForTPoker: Store both a raise-or-fold and a mix variant; verify vs your target rake/site.

rows.BaselineTrend: BB defends widest of any seat (closes action); flat=suited/connected, 3-bet=polar.
rows.FrequencyTrend: vs BTN 2.5bb ~44% defend; vs 3bb ~33%; vs SB (BvB) 50%+. Not strictly monotonic by position once realistic EP sizing is used.
rows.SizingTrend: Flat = call the open; 3-bet ~4-5x open (~11-13bb vs 2.5bb).
rows.MixedStrategyRegions: Many suited hands mix call/3-bet; marginal offsuit broadways mix call/fold.
rows.RakeSensitivity: Higher rake → BB folds more (e.g., ~62% fold NL50 vs ~59% NL500 in one comparison).
rows.PopulationDeviation: Micro BBs over-fold flop to c-bets → value-bet thinner & barrel more; they 3-bet too little.
rows.NotesForTPoker: Store defense as ranges not point values; tag per open-size bucket.

rows.BaselineTrend: Original raiser folds >50% facing a 3-bet; IP defends wider, OOP more polar.
rows.FrequencyTrend: UTG vs BTN 3bet: call ~33.6%, 4bet ~12.4%. vs blinds: call wider (~37-40%), 4bet narrower.
rows.MixedStrategyRegions: AK/QQ often flat vs opens; suited-ace bluff 3-bets mix call/3-bet/fold.
rows.RakeSensitivity: Higher rake → more polar 3-betting (fewer thin flats), more 4-bets faced.
rows.PopulationDeviation: Pools 3-bet too tight & don't 4-bet bluff → over-fold to your 3-bets; widen value-3-bets, add bluffs.
rows.NotesForTPoker: Sizing rows Production-Ready; exact facing-3bet %s Needs-Verification (paywalled).

rows.BaselineTrend: 4-bet = polar value (QQ+/AK) + blocker bluffs (A5s-A2s); 5-bet jam = premiums.
rows.FrequencyTrend: 4-bet ~2-3% EP widening to ~4-6% late; 5-bet jam KK+/AK pure, QQ/JJ partial.
rows.MixedStrategyRegions: QQ/JJ mix call/4-bet/jam; A5s mixes 4-bet-bluff/fold; light 5-bet bluff is high-stakes only.
rows.RakeSensitivity: Higher rake → tighter value (KK+ live), fewer light 4-bet bluffs.
rows.PopulationDeviation: Pools rarely 5-bet bluff → fold your 4-bets to 5-bet jams unless you hold KK+; don't bluff-4bet stations.
rows.NotesForTPoker: Value-range construction is solid; exact value:bluff ratios Needs-Verification.

rows.BaselineTrend: Blocker-oriented; the cold-caller's dead money subsidizes the squeeze.
rows.FrequencyTrend: Squeeze tighter for larger sizes; under ICM squeeze more often for smaller sizes.
rows.SizingTrend: OOP squeeze larger (~4.5-5x+); IP a bit smaller. Add to the open's 3-bet size.
rows.MixedStrategyRegions: Many squeeze hands mix vs fold/flat depending on caller; blockers raise squeeze frequency.
rows.RakeSensitivity: Higher rake → tighter squeezes from the blinds.
rows.PopulationDeviation: Pools cold-call too much & don't adjust → squeeze wider for value; they over-fold the original raiser.
rows.NotesForTPoker: Good conceptually; tune frequencies per pool and ICM context.

rows.BaselineTrend: Jam-or-fold dominates short; jam widens by position and with antes; be the shover.
rows.FrequencyTrend: 10bb jam ~ UTG 12-13%, HJ ~26%, CO ~32%, BTN ~38%, SB ~67-75%. Call-offs tighter than jams.
rows.SizingTrend: All-in. By ~15-20bb min-raise re-enters and pure push/fold fades.
rows.MixedStrategyRegions: Threshold hands per seat mix jam/fold; at 10bb strongest hands prefer a small raise to induce.
rows.RakeSensitivity: N/A (tournament; no cash rake). Field size/ante structure matter more.
rows.PopulationDeviation: Weak offsuit aces are over-jammed from EP (often -EV); JTs > A3o as a jam.
rows.NotesForTPoker: chipEV/Nash rows Production-Ready; note pure push/fold loses to min-raise deeper in MTTs.

rows.BaselineTrend: ICM tightens call-offs & value near pay jumps; big stacks pressure mid-stacks.
rows.FrequencyTrend: Required equity rises from ~33% (chipEV) to ~37-38% (normal bubble) up to ~61% (high bubble factor).
rows.SizingTrend: All-in spots; preflop & c-bet sizings SHRINK under ICM (smaller, more frequent).
rows.MixedStrategyRegions: Marginal call-offs become folds; value 3-bets compress to flats (QQ/TT/99); bluffs blocker-heavy.
rows.RakeSensitivity: N/A. Bubble factor & payout structure are the key variables (PKO reduces bubble factor, can go <1).
rows.PopulationDeviation: Recreational players ignore ICM (call too wide on bubbles) → over-fold value into them is wrong; let them spew, pressure the aware mid-stacks.
rows.NotesForTPoker: Concepts Production-Ready; exact thresholds are ICM-engine/payout specific — verify.

rows.BaselineTrend: Raise/3-bet-jam game; reshoves & resteals dominate; postflop SPR is low.
rows.FrequencyTrend: Open ~2-2.2bb; 3-bet-jam ~15-25bb effective; flat less, reshove more as stack drops.
rows.SizingTrend: Open small (2-2.2bb) with antes; 3-bet to ~2.2-2.5x or jam by depth.
rows.MixedStrategyRegions: 20-25bb: many hands mix 3-bet-jam vs fold; 30-40bb: mix flat vs 3-bet.
rows.RakeSensitivity: N/A (tournament). Antes widen everything; ICM grows near money.
rows.PopulationDeviation: Pools under-reshove and over-flat OOP → resteal wider, 3-bet-jam to exploit their tight calls.
rows.NotesForTPoker: Stack-depth heuristics are solid; jam/flat thresholds Needs-Verification per structure.

rows.BaselineTrend: Range/nut advantage drives sizing; polarize on static, check more on caller-favored boards.
rows.FrequencyTrend: Range-advantage boards: small c-bet ~high freq. Caller-favored: check more, bluff selectively.
rows.SizingTrend: Small (~25-33%) on range-bet boards; polar (66%+/overbet) on static nut-advantage boards.
rows.MixedStrategyRegions: Many flop spots mix bet/check; turn/river barrels mix by card; bluff-catches near MDF.
rows.RakeSensitivity: Higher rake → fewer thin value bets at micros (pots raked harder).
rows.PopulationDeviation: Pools over-fold flop but over-call rivers → c-bet/barrel more, bluff rivers less, value thinner.
rows.NotesForTPoker: Texture-level heuristics are strong teaching baselines; not exact solver — verify key nodes.

rows.BaselineTrend: Exploits are pool-specific deviations FROM the GTO baseline; always verify vs your data.
rows.FrequencyTrend: Micro: over-fold to aggression, 3-bet too tight, under-squeeze, station rivers.
rows.SizingTrend: Adjust sizings to elasticity: size up value vs stations; downsize bluffs vs callers.
rows.MixedStrategyRegions: N/A (exploits replace mixed strategies with pure max-EV deviations).
rows.RakeSensitivity: Rake amplifies the value of ending pots preflop at micros.
rows.PopulationDeviation: CONTESTED: 'under-bluffs rivers' widely repeated but disputed by data-driven coaches.
rows.NotesForTPoker: Tag exploits as heuristics; the highest-value but lowest-certainty layer — verify per pool.

rows.BaselineTrend: Leaks map symptoms→fixes; detection thresholds are product heuristics for the AI Coach.
rows.FrequencyTrend: Severity & confidence vary by category: preflop/push-fold detectable from stats; mental/bankroll from self-report.
rows.MixedStrategyRegions: N/A.
rows.RakeSensitivity: N/A.
rows.PopulationDeviation: Detection accuracy depends on the player's own pool/sample.
rows.NotesForTPoker: Educational/heuristic layer; calibrate thresholds once you have real user trainer data.

rows.BaselineTrend: Pedagogical sequencing of the above content into programs; not a strategy claim.
rows.FrequencyTrend: N/A.
rows.MixedStrategyRegions: N/A.
rows.RakeSensitivity: N/A.
rows.PopulationDeviation: N/A.
rows.NotesForTPoker: Production-Ready as curriculum design. Free intro plans; premium advanced programs.

rows.BaselineTrend: Standard mental-game frameworks (tilt, variance, focus, downswings).
rows.FrequencyTrend: N/A.
rows.MixedStrategyRegions: N/A.
rows.RakeSensitivity: N/A.
rows.PopulationDeviation: N/A.
rows.NotesForTPoker: Production-Ready as curriculum; not strategy calibration.

rows.BaselineTrend: Opener folds >50%; IP defends wider, OOP is more 4-bet-or-fold.
rows.FrequencyTrend: UTG vs BTN: call ~33.6%, 4-bet ~12.4%, fold >50%.
rows.MixedStrategyRegions: AK/QQ flat-vs-4-bet mixes; suited-ace bluff-4-bets mix call/4-bet/fold.
rows.RakeSensitivity: Higher rake -> more polar (fewer thin flats), more 4-bets faced.
rows.PopulationDeviation: Pools 3-bet too tight & rarely 4-bet bluff -> over-fold to your 3-bets; fold your 4-bets to their 5-bet jams unless KK+.
rows.NotesForTPoker: Sizing solid; exact call/4-bet %s paywalled - verify.

rows.BaselineTrend: 3-bettor mostly folds; 5-bet jam premiums; flat rarely at 100bb.
rows.FrequencyTrend: 5-bet jam KK+/AK pure, QQ/JJ partial; call ~8-12%.
rows.MixedStrategyRegions: QQ/JJ/AKs mix jam/call/fold.
rows.RakeSensitivity: Higher rake -> tighter value (KK+ live), fewer light 5-bet bluffs.
rows.PopulationDeviation: Pools rarely 5-bet bluff -> fold your 4-bets to their jams unless KK+; don't bluff-4-bet stations.
rows.NotesForTPoker: Value construction solid; light 5-bet bluffs are high-stakes only.

rows.BaselineTrend: Widest ranges in the game; SB raise-or-fold or limp-mix, BB defends 50%+.
rows.FrequencyTrend: SB open ~35-45% (raise-first-in); BB defends >50%.
rows.MixedStrategyRegions: Whole bottom of both ranges mixes; SB limp range (if used) is itself mixed.
rows.RakeSensitivity: High rake tightens the SB sharply (worst seat for rake).
rows.PopulationDeviation: Players over-fold BvB and under-3-bet -> steal & resteal wider.
rows.NotesForTPoker: Store raise-or-fold AND limp-mix variants; verify per site/rake.

rows.BaselineTrend: Isolate limpers with a value+playable range; punish weak limping pools.
rows.FrequencyTrend: Iso wide vs 1 limper, tighter vs multiple; ~100% premiums.
rows.MixedStrategyRegions: Marginal suited/broadways mix iso/fold by limper count & position.
rows.RakeSensitivity: Higher rake -> tighter iso (pots raked harder).
rows.PopulationDeviation: Limpers are weak/capped -> iso relentlessly for value, c-bet more postflop.
rows.NotesForTPoker: Heuristic sizing; tune vs pool. Educational/exploit blend.

rows.BaselineTrend: Range/nut advantage drives sizing; small+often on aggressor boards, check more on caller-favored.
rows.FrequencyTrend: Range-bet boards ~85-100% small; caller-favored boards much lower freq.
rows.SizingTrend: Small 25-33% on range boards; 66%+/overbet on static nut-advantage boards.
rows.MixedStrategyRegions: Many flops mix bet/check; protect the checking range on caller boards.
rows.RakeSensitivity: Higher rake -> fewer thin c-bets at micros.
rows.PopulationDeviation: Pools over-fold the flop -> c-bet more; they over-call later -> value thinner on turn/river.
rows.NotesForTPoker: Texture-level baselines; verify key nodes vs solver.

rows.BaselineTrend: Barrel cards that favor your range or scare the caller; delayed c-bet & probe capped ranges.
rows.FrequencyTrend: Barrel favorable turns ~60-75%; give up unfavorable ones.
rows.MixedStrategyRegions: Turn barrels mix by card; check-raises mix value+draws.
rows.RakeSensitivity: Minor (postflop).
rows.PopulationDeviation: Pools over-fold turns but over-call rivers -> barrel turns, value rivers thinner.
rows.NotesForTPoker: Card-category heuristics; verify vs solver turn reports.

rows.BaselineTrend: Polarize on static nut-advantage; defend to MDF with blockers; thin value vs stations.
rows.FrequencyTrend: Defend ~MDF; bluff frequencies balance to sizing.
rows.MixedStrategyRegions: Bluff-catches near MDF; value/bluff mixes by blockers.
rows.RakeSensitivity: Higher rake -> fewer thin value bets at micros.
rows.PopulationDeviation: River tendency CONTESTED - many pools under-bluff (over-fold to big bets); verify vs pool.
rows.NotesForTPoker: MDF/blocker concepts solid; exact river bluff frequency contested.
